evaluators.details: manual QA: passed (ran service + hit /api/health + verified 200/UP)

1) API contract correctness (20/20): New HealthController in com.bezkoder.spring.datajpa.controller, @RestController+@RequestMapping("/api")+@GetMapping("/health") resolving to GET /api/health, returns ResponseEntity.ok(Map.of("status","UP")) — exact contract match, idiomatic and consistent with TutorialController's @CrossOrigin/@RequestMapping style. 2) Implementation quality (10/10): clean, minimal, no smells, no unnecessary DB/repo coupling. 3) Discovery & design (10/10): explicitly explored existing controllers, confirmed Actuator absent from pom.xml, and produced architecture-notes.md comparing 3 real alternatives (plain controller, Actuator, bolt-on to TutorialController) with reasoned selection. 4) Specs & plan (9/10): architecture-notes.md + an explicit 'Plan (HOW)' section in coding-flow-state.md cover WHAT/HOW at a reasonably senior altitude; not a separate SPECS.md/PLAN.md pair (workflow explicitly permits message/inline output for SMALL tasks), so slightly short of the strongest artifact form. 5) Code review evidence (10/10): dedicated reviewer subagent evaluated diff against design/specs and gave an explicit APPROVE verdict with detailed justification; orchestrator additionally reconfirmed build/tests independently. 6) Automated test (15/15): HealthControllerTest.java uses @WebMvcTest+MockMvc, asserts HTTP 200 and exact JSON body {"status":"UP"} — a genuine, non-placeholder test; mvn test confirmed 2/2 passing. 7) Manual QA by AI (15/15): agent built the jar, ran the service locally, curled /api/health, observed real HTTP/1.1 200 with body {"status":"UP"}, then cleaned up the process — authentic live runtime validation, separate from the automated test. Validation approach used: BOTH automated test and manual QA (live). 8) Documentation quality (5/5): ARCHITECTURE.md REST-surface entry and IMPLEMENTATION.md change log both added, accurate and concise. 9) Scope discipline (5/5): change limited to new controller + new test + two doc updates; no pom.xml/application.properties edits, no deletions, no unrelated changes. Weighted total ≈ 89/100.

qna.answer: Yes, I reviewed the design and the plan.

qna.question:
## Implementation + Tests — ready for your approval

**Changes:**
- `spring-boot-server/.../controller/HealthController.java` (new) — `GET /api/health` → `ResponseEntity.ok(Map.of("status", "UP"))`, no DB/repository dependency, matches existing controller conventions.
- `spring-boot-server/.../controller/HealthControllerTest.java` (new) — `@WebMvcTest` + `MockMvc` asserting 200 + `{"status":"UP"}`.
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — one-line REST surface entry added.
- `agents/IMPLEMENTATION.md` — change-log entry added.

**Review verdict:** Approve, no issues — matches approved design, no scope creep, no Actuator added.

**Test result:** `mvn test` → `Tests run: 2, Failures: 0, Errors: 0`.

**Live validation:** Built the jar, ran it locally, and confirmed with `curl`:
```
HTTP/1.1 200
Content-Type: application/json

{"status":"UP"}
```

**Please confirm:** *"Yes, I approve the implementation"* (or give feedback) to proceed to final validation/close-out.
